---
url: /handbook/schedule.md
description: Manage schedule items with due-date reminders and calendar sync.
---

# Schedule

On the schedule page, you can view all schedule items, and add or delete them.

## Nearest Schedule

When you enter the schedule page, the nearest upcoming (non-expired) schedule item is displayed at the top, including the name, time, location, and a countdown to the start time. If the item is linked to a course, the course name is also shown.

Tap the nearest schedule card to view its details.

## Schedule Groups

You can create groups to organize your schedule items. Groups are shown above the schedule list; tap a group to filter items in that group. Each group can have a custom name and icon.

## Add a Schedule Item

Tap the "+" button in the top-right corner to add a new item. When adding, you can set:

* **Name**: The title of the item (required)
* **Location**: Where the event takes place
* **Notes**: Additional details
* **Target time**: The start time of the event
* **End time**: Optional; the end time (must be within 24 hours of the start time)
* **Group**: Assign the item to a group
* **Reminder**: Get notified before the event starts — options include 10 minutes, 30 minutes, 1 hour, 2 hours, 4 hours, or 8 hours
* **Repeat**: Set a repeat rule — daily, weekly, or monthly — with a configurable end date
* **Linked course**: Associate the item with a course from your timetable

## Edit and Delete

Tap a schedule item in the list to view its details. In the detail popup, you can:

* Tap the settings button to edit the item
* Tap the delete button to remove it
* If the item has a repeat rule, you can choose to delete all related repeat items

## System Calendar Integration (iOS)

After adding or editing a schedule item, Ham automatically syncs it to the system calendar (calendar name: "Ham-日程"). The item's name, location, notes, time, and reminder are all synced.

## Notification Reminders

If you set a reminder, Ham sends a system notification before the event starts. The notification includes the item name, location, and start time.

## Required Permissions

### Schedule Reminders

* iOS: Notifications, Calendar
* Android: Notification permission

---

---
url: /developers/ham-rn/score-calc.md
description: GPA and credit calculation utilities using custom JavaScript scripts.
---

# Score Calculator Module

## User Entry Point

**Me → Scores → Settings → F2 Calculation Method → Use other calculation (JavaScript-based) → Choose calculation**

Users go to the "Me" page, enter Scores, tap Settings, under "F2 calculation method" select "Use other calculation (JavaScript-based)", then tap "Choose calculation" to enter the score calculation script selection page, which is rendered by Ham React Native Components.

## Features

The score calculator module provides custom GPA / weighted score calculation based on JavaScript. Users can:

1. Browse available calculation scripts (from GitHub and other sources)
2. Select and apply a calculation script
3. View script details (author, version, update notes, etc.)
4. Test whether a script runs correctly

## Registered Entry

| Registration Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `RNScoreCalcView` | Component | Score calculation script selection view |

## Code Structure

### Business Logic (`business/education/scorecalc`)

* `fetch.ts` — Fetches available calculation scripts from remote sources
* `type.ts` — Type definitions (calculation script metadata structure)

### UI Components (`components/scorecalc`)

* `ScoreCalcView.tsx` — Score calculator main view, containing sub-components:
  * Current score card — Displays the currently selected calculation method
  * Description cell — Shows script description and update notes
  * Developer card — Displays script author information
  * GitHub link card — Links to the script's GitHub repository

## Workflow

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant User as User
    participant App as Ham Native App
    participant RN as ham-rn (RNScoreCalcView)
    participant GitHub as Remote Script Source

    User->>App: Me → Scores → Settings → Use other calculation (JavaScript-based) → Choose calculation
    App->>RN: Load RNScoreCalcView component
    RN->>RN: Get current script via NativeScoreCalcModule.getCurrentCalc
    RN->>GitHub: Fetch available calculation scripts
    GitHub-->>RN: Return script list (with metadata and code)
    RN->>User: Display script list
    User->>RN: Select a script
    RN->>RN: Test script via NativeScoreCalcModule.testItem
    RN->>App: Apply script via NativeScoreCalcModule.selectCalc
    App->>App: Recalculate GPA using new script
```

## Calculation Script Format

A calculation script is a JavaScript function that receives a score list JSON string and user info JSON string, returning an array:

```javascript
/**
 * @param {string} scoreListJson - Score list JSON string
 * @param {string} userInfoJson - User info JSON string
 * @returns {[number, string[]]} - [calculation result, selected course ID list]
 */
function calc(scoreListJson, userInfoJson) {
    const scoreList = JSON.parse(scoreListJson);
    const userInfo = JSON.parse(userInfoJson);
    // Custom calculation logic
    return [score, selectedCourseIds];
}
```

## How to Add a New Calculation Method

### Step 1: Create a Calculation Script

Create a new `.ts` file under `src/business/education/scorecalc/embed/` and use the `defineEmbed` function to define your calculation logic:

```typescript
import {defineEmbed} from '@/business/education/scorecalc/defineEmbed';

defineEmbed((scoreList, userInfo) => {
  // scoreList: ScoreJsItem[] — list of course scores
  // userInfo: UserInfo — user information

  // Write your calculation logic here
  let totalWeighted = 0;
  let totalCredit = 0;
  const selectedIds: string[] = [];

  for (const item of scoreList) {
    totalWeighted += item.credit * item.score;
    totalCredit += item.credit;
    selectedIds.push(item.courseId);
  }

  const result = totalCredit > 0 ? totalWeighted / totalCredit : 0;

  // Return [calculated score, list of course IDs used in calculation]
  return [result, selectedIds];
});
```

### Step 2: Available Fields

Each element (`ScoreJsItem`) in `scoreList` contains the following fields:

| Field | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `courseType` | `string` | Course category (e.g. "公共基础必修") |
| `name` | `string` | Course name (e.g. "高等数学") |
| `credit` | `number` | Credit hours |
| `courseCollege` | `string` | College offering the course |
| `instructor` | `string` | Instructor name |
| `score` | `number` | Numeric score |
| `courseId` | `string` | Unique course identifier |

`userInfo` (`UserInfo`) contains the following fields:

| Field | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `userCollege` | `string` | User's college (e.g. "计算机学院") |

### Step 3: Register the Script

Add a new entry in the `fetchScoreCalcFromLocal` function in `src/business/education/scorecalc/fetch.ts`:

```typescript
import newScript from './embed/generated/your-script.generated';

const fetchScoreCalcFromLocal = (): Array<ScoreCalcItem> => {
  return [
    // ... existing entries
    {
      title: 'Your Calculation Method Name',
      date: '2026-01-01',
      author: 'Author Name',
      version: 1,
      brief: 'Short description',
      updateBrief: 'Update notes',
      desc: 'Detailed description',
      type: 'APP',
      url: 'https://raw.githubusercontent.com/whu-ham/ham-rn/main/src/business/education/scorecalc/embed/your-script.ts',
      script: newScript,
    },
  ];
};
```

### Notes

* `defineEmbed` registers your calculation function on `globalThis.calc`, making it callable from the native JSContext (e.g. iOS JavaScriptCore).
* The return value must be in `[number, string[]]` format — the first element is the calculated score, the second is the list of course IDs that contributed to the calculation.
* `.ts` files in the `embed/` directory are compiled into corresponding `.generated` files at build time, which are then imported by `fetch.ts`.
* You can filter courses by `courseType` (e.g. required vs. elective), or implement college-specific logic using `userCollege`.

## Related Native Modules

| Module | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `NativeScoreCalcModule` | Score calculation script management (get current / select / view details / test scripts) |

---

---
url: /handbook/sport.md
description: How to book sports facilities with Ham.
---

# Sports Facilities

::: tip Version requirements
iOS: 1.3.2 (build >= 96)

Android: 1.4.3
:::

You can book sports facilities with Ham.

## Preparation

Before booking, log in to your information portal account in "Me" -> "Information Portal Settings."

## Browse facilities

When browsing facilities, you can select your sport and venue, then check available slots and times to book.

Browsing facilities is suitable when:

* You suddenly want to work out and have no booking
* A venue has plenty of remaining slots

## Favorite bookings

You can set favorite venues, sessions, and time slots in settings. After you set them, the booking appears on the home page for quick booking.
::: tip Note
Quick booking on the home page does not confirm availability for the slot. If a booking fails, Ham will select a nearby available slot automatically.
:::

## Order center

On the home page, you can go to the order center to view or cancel your orders.

## Pay for orders

When a booking succeeds and payment is due, you can pay in two ways:

* Pay via WeChat mini program:

Pay in the "Venue Booking" mini program. This option supports WeChat Pay.

* Pay on the web:

After confirming the details, Ham provides a link to the university payment platform. Copy it to a browser to complete payment. This option supports Alipay or UnionPay.

::: warning Important
Payments on the WHU payment platform are not handled by Ham.
:::

## Booking status

Your booking status is shown on the home page.

When payment is due, you can pay from the booking status card.

---

---
url: /developers/ham-web/sso-authorize.md
description: SSO OAuth2 authorization flow and API reference for Ham Web.
---

# SSO OAuth2 Authorization

## User Entry Point

When third-party apps initiate OAuth2 authorization through the Ham Connect platform, users are redirected to Ham Web's `/sso-authorize` page.

The page behavior depends on the user's device and login status:

* **Mobile**: Attempts to launch the Ham native app via `ham://sso-authorize?...` Deep Link first. Falls back to web login if the launch fails
* **Desktop**: Displays the web login page directly

## Features

The SSO OAuth2 authorization page handles the following flow:

1. Parse URL parameters (`client_id`, `scope`, `redirect_uri`, `state`)
2. Detect device type (mobile / desktop)
3. Attempt Deep Link launch on mobile
4. If web login is needed, provide the following login methods:
   * **QR Code Login** — Scan with the Ham app to log in
   * **Passkey Login** — Passwordless login via WebAuthn / Passkey
5. After login, display the consent page (showing third-party app info and requested scopes)
6. After user confirms, redirect back to the third-party app's `redirect_uri` with an authorization code

## Page Stages

The page uses Jotai state management and transitions through the following stages:

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> loading: Parse URL params
    loading --> invalid: Invalid params
    loading --> deep_link_trying: Mobile
    loading --> login: Desktop (not logged in)
    loading --> consent: Desktop (logged in)
    deep_link_trying --> deep_link_fallback: Launch failed
    deep_link_fallback --> login: Choose web login
    login --> consent: Login success
    consent --> [*]: Authorized, redirect
```

| Stage | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `loading` | Parsing URL parameters, detecting device type |
| `invalid` | Invalid URL parameters, showing error page |
| `deep-link-trying` | Attempting to launch the Ham native app |
| `deep-link-fallback` | Launch failed, offering app install links and web login options |
| `login` | Web login page (QR code / Passkey) |
| `consent` | Authorization consent page |

## Code Structure

### Page Components (`app/sso-authorize/`)

* `page.client.tsx` — Main page component, handles stage transitions and Deep Link logic
* `store.ts` — Jotai state atoms (URL params, page stage, Deep Link URL)
* `LoginView.tsx` — Login view (QR code + Passkey tabs)
* `ConsentView.tsx` — Authorization consent view
* `DeepLinkTrying.tsx` — Deep Link attempt view
* `DeepLinkFallback.tsx` — Deep Link fallback view
* `HeaderBar.tsx` — Page header bar
* `InvalidRequestView.tsx` — Invalid request view

### Service Layer (`services/sso/`)

* `api.ts` — SSO API wrapper (QR login, Passkey login, session management, consent)
* `deepLink.ts` — Deep Link construction and launch logic
* `ua.ts` — Device type detection (mobile / desktop)

### API Routes (`app/api/`)

* `auth/qr/ticket/` — QR login ticket creation and polling
* `auth/passkey/` — Passkey login (get options, verify assertion)
* `auth/me/` — Get current logged-in user info
* `auth/logout/` — Logout
* `auth/refresh/` — Refresh session
* `sso/consent/info/` — Get consent info (third-party app name, scope descriptions)
* `sso/consent/confirm/` — Confirm authorization, return authorization code

## Workflow

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant ThirdParty as Third-party App
    participant Browser as Browser
    participant HamWeb as Ham Web
    participant HamBackend as Ham Backend
    participant HamApp as Ham Native App

    ThirdParty->>Browser: Redirect to /sso-authorize?client_id=...
    Browser->>HamWeb: Load authorization page
    
    alt Mobile
        HamWeb->>HamApp: Attempt Deep Link launch
        alt Launch successful
            HamApp->>HamApp: Complete authorization in-app
            HamApp->>ThirdParty: Redirect to redirect_uri
        else Launch failed
            HamWeb->>Browser: Show web login page
        end
    end
    
    alt Web login
        Browser->>HamWeb: Choose login method (QR code / Passkey)
        HamWeb->>HamBackend: Authentication request
        HamBackend-->>HamWeb: Auth success, set session cookie
        HamWeb->>HamBackend: Query consent info
        HamBackend-->>HamWeb: Return app info and scopes
        HamWeb->>Browser: Show consent page
        Browser->>HamWeb: User confirms authorization
        HamWeb->>HamBackend: Submit consent confirmation
        HamBackend-->>HamWeb: Return authorization code
        HamWeb->>ThirdParty: Redirect to redirect_uri?code=...&state=...
    end
```

## Session Management

The authorization page implements a visibility-aware session refresh mechanism:

* While on the consent stage, listens for `visibilitychange` events
* Each time the page returns to the foreground, calls `/auth/refresh` to renew the session cookie
* If the session has expired (401), automatically redirects the user back to the login stage

---

---
url: /handbook/status.md
description: 'Status page showing today''s tasks, weather, library reservations, and classes.'
---

# Status

The status page shows the tasks you need to handle today.

### Weather

Shows real-time weather so you can adjust what you wear.

### Library Reservation

Shows your current reservations. You can also change the reservation time quickly.

### Classes

Shows your courses for today or tomorrow.

### Schedule

Shows the schedule items you added in Ham.

### Campus Bus

Shows campus bus info for the nearest stop.

### Sports

Shows your booked sports venues.

---

---
url: /privacy/user-policy.md
description: Ham user agreement - terms of service.
---

# User Agreement

This agreement sets out the rights and obligations between Ham and users for Ham software services (the "Service"). "User" refers to any individual who downloads, installs, registers, logs in, or uses the Service (collectively, "use"). Before registering, Ham hereby reminds you to read this User Agreement (the "Agreement") carefully to ensure you fully understand all terms. Please read and understand the terms carefully, especially those related to limitation or exclusion of liability, rights licensing and information use, consent to special services, applicable law, and dispute resolution. Important terms such as exclusions or limitations of liability will be highlighted in bold. If you do not agree to this Agreement, Ham cannot provide complete products and services, and you may choose to stop using the Service. If you choose to agree or use Ham and related services, you are deemed to have fully understood and accepted this Agreement and other agreements and rules related to Ham (including but not limited to this Agreement).

To provide better services, or due to changes in laws, policies, technology, or product features, Ham may revise this Agreement from time to time. Revisions form part of this Agreement. After updates, Ham will notify users via the official website announcement or other appropriate ways. Users can also view the latest version on the website homepage or in app settings. Continued use of Ham and related services indicates acceptance of the revised Agreement.

## 1. Account Registration

1. Users must register a Ham account before using the Service. Ham may change account registration or binding methods based on user needs or product requirements.

2. Given Ham account binding methods, you agree that Ham may use information such as social accounts or phone numbers provided by you during registration.

3. You understand and agree that account information (name, avatar, profile, and other personal information) must comply with laws and Ham rules. It must not contain illegal or inappropriate content, and must not impersonate others (including using another person's name, title, avatar, etc.) or otherwise cause confusion. You must not maliciously register accounts (including frequent or batch registrations). You must comply with laws and regulations and not engage in behavior that harms national interests, infringes on others' legal rights, or violates public morals. Ham has the right to review registration information.

4. During registration and use, Ham may collect personal information needed to identify you or to provide better service. This may include your name, gender, age, date of birth, ID number, address, school, company, industry, interests, frequent locations, and personal profile. Ham's use of this information is subject to the personal information protection provisions in Section 3.

## 2. Service Content

1. The specific content of the Service is provided by Ham based on actual conditions, including but not limited to horoscope and weather queries. To improve user experience, Ham may change its services from time to time (including software modifications, upgrades, feature enhancements, new services, or replacements). Users will be notified of such changes (including system prompts, announcements, or in-app messages). Users may choose to accept updates; if you do not update, some features may be limited or unavailable.

2. Ham grants users a personal, revocable, non-transferable, non-exclusive, non-commercial license to use Ham software and related services. All other rights not expressly granted remain with Ham. Before exercising those rights, you must obtain written permission from Ham. Failure to exercise a right does not constitute a waiver.

3. Ham services include free and paid services. Users may purchase paid services via online banking, Alipay, or other payment methods provided by Ham. For paid services, Ham will provide clear notice before use. Only after you agree to pay and complete payment can you use paid services. Completion is determined by the "payment completed" confirmation from the bank or third-party payment platform.

## 3. Protection of User Personal Information

1. During registration or use, users may be required to submit certain necessary personal information as required by laws and regulations. If submitted information is incomplete or non-compliant, you may be unable to use the Service or may be restricted during use.

2. User personal information includes: (1) information provided by the user (such as phone number, location when enabling location services); (2) information shared by other parties; and (3) information lawfully collected by Ham to provide services (such as device or software information, browsing history, and time data). Personal information refers to any information recorded electronically or otherwise that can identify a specific natural person alone or in combination with other information. You understand and agree that Ham will process and protect personal information in accordance with the Privacy Policy published by Ham. You are encouraged to read and understand the Privacy Policy in full and make appropriate choices as needed.

3. Ham values personal information protection and will take technical and other necessary measures to keep it secure and prevent leakage, damage, or loss. If such an incident occurs or is likely to occur, Ham will take remedial measures and inform the user. Users who discover such issues should contact Ham immediately.

4. Ham will not disclose user personal information to any third party without your consent, except:
   * When required by laws or competent authorities;
   * When personal information is disclosed due to the user sharing account credentials or other non-Ham causes;
   * When the user voluntarily discloses personal information to a third party;
   * When the user and Ham (and partners) publicly agree on usage and Ham discloses information accordingly;
   * When personal information is disclosed due to hacking, computer viruses, or force majeure;
   * When personal information has been processed so it cannot identify a specific person and cannot be restored.

5. Users agree that Ham may use personal information for:
   * Sending important notices such as software updates and agreement changes;
   * Internal audits, data analysis, and research to improve products and communication;
   * Managing, reviewing, and processing user information according to this Agreement;
   * Other matters required by applicable laws and regulations.

Except as above, Ham will not use personal information for any other purpose without prior consent.

6. Ham values the protection of minors. Ham relies on user-provided information to determine whether a user is a minor. Anyone under 18 may not register an account or use the Service.

7. Ham will collect, use, or disclose personal information lawfully, properly, and as necessary, and will not collect information unrelated to the Service.

8. Ham has a Privacy Policy. Users should read and understand it before using Ham products and/or services. If you disagree with the policy, the Service may not function properly or as intended. You can also view it in the "About" section in settings. Ham hopes the Privacy Policy clearly explains how personal information is handled, and recommends reading it in full to better protect your privacy.

## 4. Content Guidelines

1. "Content" refers to any content created, uploaded, copied, published, or transmitted by users while using the Service, including account avatars, names, profiles, and other registration information or verification data, as well as text, audio, images, videos, and other content, and related links or pages.

2. Users must not use Ham accounts or the Service to create, upload, copy, publish, or transmit content prohibited by laws, regulations, or policies, including but not limited to:
   * Opposing the basic principles established by the Constitution;
   * Endangering national security, leaking state secrets, subverting state power, or undermining national unity;
   * Damaging national honor or interests;
   * Distorting, vilifying, or denying heroic deeds and spirit, or infringing on a hero's name, portrait, reputation, or honor by insult or defamation;
   * Promoting terrorism, extremism, or inciting terrorist or extremist activities;
   * Inciting ethnic hatred or discrimination, or undermining ethnic unity;
   * Undermining national religious policies, promoting cults or superstition;
   * Spreading rumors or disrupting economic or social order;
   * Spreading obscenity, pornography, gambling, violence, murder, terror, or abetting crime;
   * Insulting or defaming others, or infringing on others' reputation, privacy, or legal rights;
   * Inciting illegal assembly, association, marching, demonstration, or gathering to disrupt public order;
   * Acting in the name of illegal civil organizations;
   * Violating the "seven bottom lines" requirements of laws and regulations, the socialist system, national interests, citizens' legal rights, public order, moral standards, and authenticity of information;
   * Any other content prohibited by laws, administrative regulations, or policies.

3. Users must not use Ham accounts or the Service to create, upload, copy, publish, or transmit content that interferes with Ham operations or infringes on others' legal rights, including but not limited to:
   * Exaggerated headlines that are inconsistent with the content;
   * Hype of scandals or negative news;
   * Improper commentary on disasters or major incidents;
   * Sexual hints or suggestive content;
   * Graphic, scary, or brutal content that causes discomfort;
   * Inciting discrimination or regional discrimination;
   * Promoting vulgar, lowbrow, or kitsch content;
   * Content that may induce minors to imitate unsafe behavior or form bad habits;
   * Insults, threats, or harassment;
   * Spam, malicious, or deceptive information;
   * Infringing others' personal information or data;
   * Infringing others' portrait rights, intellectual property, trade secrets, or other legal rights;
   * Other content that interferes with normal operations or harms users or third parties.

## 5. Usage Rules

1. Any content transmitted or published by users through the Service does not reflect Ham's views or policies, and Ham assumes no responsibility for it.

2. Users must not use Ham accounts or the Service to engage in any of the following:
   * Submit or publish false information, or impersonate others by using their avatars or information;
   * Force or induce users to follow, click links, or share information;
   * Mislead or deceive others by fabricating facts or concealing truth;
   * Use technical means to batch-create fake accounts;
   * Engage in illegal or criminal activities using the Service;
   * Produce or distribute methods or tools related to the above behaviors, or operate or spread them, whether or not for commercial purposes;
   * Other acts that violate laws or regulations, infringe others' legal rights, or interfere with Ham operations.

3. Users are fully responsible for the authenticity, legality, harmlessness, accuracy, and effectiveness of content transmitted through their accounts. Any legal liability arising from such content is borne by the user. If Ham or third parties suffer losses, the user shall compensate accordingly.

4. The Service may include advertisements. Users agree to display ads from Ham and third-party providers or partners during use. Unless explicitly stated by laws, users are responsible for any transactions conducted based on such ads. Ham bears no responsibility for losses caused by such transactions or the advertising content.

5. Unless permitted in writing by Ham, users must not:
   * Delete copyright information on the software or copies;
   * Reverse engineer, decompile, disassemble, or otherwise attempt to discover the source code;
   * Use, rent, lend, copy, modify, link, reprint, compile, publish, or build mirror sites for content with Ham intellectual property rights;
   * Copy, modify, add, delete, or create derivative works of data in memory, client-server interaction data, or system data required for software operation, including through plugins or unauthorized third-party tools/services;
   * Modify or forge instructions or data during software operation, change software functions or effects, or operate or disseminate tools or methods for such purposes, whether or not for commercial purposes;
   * Log in or use Ham services through unauthorized third-party software, plugins, or systems, or produce, publish, or spread such software, plugins, or systems.

## 6. Account Management

1. Ham accounts are owned by Ham. After registration, users obtain the right to use the account, which belongs only to the initial registrant. Transfer, lending, rental, assignment, or sale is prohibited. If Ham finds or reasonably suspects the account user is not the original registrant, it may suspend or terminate service and permanently disable the account to protect account security.

2. Users can (1) view and edit profile information, (2) view, modify, delete, or cancel account information via "Settings." Note that deleting information also removes stored text and images.

3. Users must properly safeguard account information and passwords. Losses caused by poor protection are the user's responsibility. Users are legally responsible for actions under their account. Users must not use another user's account or password. If you suspect unauthorized use, you must notify Ham immediately.

4. Users must comply with this Agreement and use the Service correctly. If a user violates any terms, Ham may suspend or terminate services for the account after notice.

5. Users can cancel their accounts via "Settings" -> "Sign Out" (entry points may change without notice). Ham will assist with cancellation after verifying identity, security status, device information, and infringement complaints, and will delete user account information as requested unless otherwise required by law. Account cancellation is irreversible. Users must back up data related to their Ham account and ensure services are properly handled. Cancellation does not exempt the account from responsibilities prior to cancellation. If during cancellation the account is subject to complaint or investigation, Ham may terminate the cancellation process at its discretion.

## 7. Risk Assumption

You understand and agree that Ham only provides a platform for information sharing, transmission, and access. You are responsible for all actions under your account, including content transmitted and any consequences. You should judge the content in Ham and the Service on your own and bear all risks, including risks arising from reliance on correctness, completeness, or usefulness. Ham does not and will not bear any responsibility for losses or damages caused by user behavior.

---

---
url: /handbook/user-center.md
description: Manage your Ham account and settings.
---

# User Center

After logging in to your Ham account, you can manage your account information and settings in the User Center.

## Ham Account

Ham supports the following login methods:

* Passkey
* QQ
* WeChat
* GitHub
* Ziqiang Auth
* Apple Sign-In (iOS only)

## Profile

After logging in, you can change your avatar and nickname in the User Center.

## Login Devices

You can view the list of currently logged-in devices and remove any devices you no longer use.

## Social Accounts

You can view your linked social accounts, link new ones, or unlink existing ones. The supported social account types are the same as the login methods.

## Passkey Management

You can manage your Passkeys here, including adding new Passkeys or removing existing ones. Passkey is a passwordless login method that uses your device's biometric authentication (such as Face ID or fingerprint) for identity verification.

## QR Code Login

You can use the app to scan a login QR code on Ham Web to quickly log in.

## Authorized Apps

You can view and manage third-party apps that have been authorized to log in with your Ham account. If you no longer use an app, you can revoke its authorization.

## More

In "More," you can perform actions such as account deactivation.
